title: Port Helix Navigators
content:
Noam Gallant (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as franchise player of the Port Helix Navigators when they won the Continental Basketball Finals in 1877.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Noam Gallant recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1877 playoffs.

Professional career.

Noam Gallant joined the Port Helix Navigators and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1872s. By 1876, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Awards and honors.

Following the Continental Basketball Finals, Noam Gallant received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Championship season.

The 1877 Continental Basketball Finals campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Noam Gallant averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Tallmere Memorial Arena in Tallmere.

Personal life.

Noam Gallant retired to Tallmere and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1879.

Playing style.

As franchise player, Noam Gallant preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Port Helix Navigators switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Legacy.

Historians of the Port Helix Navigators cite Noam Gallant's 1877 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Tallmere Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Continental Basketball Finals, Noam Gallant remained with the Port Helix Navigators for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Early life and college career.

Noam Gallant played college basketball in Tallmere before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as franchise player.
